note: 58-year-old male presenting with acute onset chest pain radiating to left arm for 2 hours. ST elevation noted on ECG in leads V1-V4. Troponin elevated. Diagnosis: anterior STEMI. Background history of hypertension and type 2 diabetes.
45-year-old female with worsening shortness of breath and productive cough for 5 days. Chest X-ray shows right lower lobe consolidation. Temperature 38.9C. Diagnosis: community-acquired pneumonia.
67-year-old male with known COPD presenting with increased dyspnoea, purulent sputum, and reduced peak flow. Acute exacerbation of COPD. Also has poorly controlled hypertension on amlodipine.
